threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it Dalston Junction; locals just say 'Junction' and know exactly what you mean. It’s the small things that give you away.
Caught the N35 last night; two guys were arguing about who saw the first ghost in Brixton Tube - you wouldn’t believe their stories!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the smell of fresh kebab mixed with the sound of street musicians became your daily soundtrack, you knew you were home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a bar that serves cocktails in giant plastic cups, perfect for sipping while you stumble between street festivals.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they can spot a tourist by their hesitation at the 16th Street BART exit and their bewilderment at the murals.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni delayed at Civic Center yesterday because a dog decided to take a nap on the tracks. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia’s plazas are just for selfies. Locals know they're where community deals, debates, and real connections happen—especially after dark.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new when they still look puzzled at the trash schedule; locals know the art of sorting recyclables before sundown.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Ordering a flat white and forgetting to specify 'no sugar'—you'll get that sweetened stuff you never asked for. Classic rookie error.
Last week on the 77, a man serenaded the entire bus with opera. No one clapped, but I've never seen people smile so wide at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy painting a giant mural of a cat on an abandoned building while kids played footie on the sidewalk. That’s Neukölln for you!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find a currywurst stall open at 4 AM serving locals after a night out—only in Berlin does a sausage taste better at sunrise.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local if they can guess the wait time for the 14-Muni based on the mood of the crowd at the bus stop.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing sax on the 19 today while a dog in a cowboy hat looked on. Only in SF does public transit feel like a mini festival.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'paseo' means strolling leisurely, but locals know it’s code for dodging skateboarders and café tables!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking the escalators instead of walking the stairs at Diagonal metro. You can spot them a mile away.
